title: alnus glutinosa subsp. barbata
intro: Alnus glutinosa subsp. barbata, also known as the narrow-leaved alder, is a subspecies of the black alder tree that is native to Europe and Asia. The narrow-leaved alder has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving cardiovascular health, due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The narrow-leaved alder can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional remedies to enhance its medicinal effects.
